In view of the above cliscussion, tliis Court is of the view that both the oourts below l-lave very ably appreciated the evidence allcJ after due appreciation, reached at their conclusion' The concurrent findings' in my l-rumble view, dicl not sllffer any iliegatity or perversity' calling for any interference by this Court while exercising the writ jurisdiction' It is further held that collstitutional petition cannot be resorted to challenge an order on the grouncl that evidence was not properly appreciatecl as findings of, the lacts recorded by the Court of competent jurisdiction cannot be disturbed soleiy on the ground that another view could be possible on the sartle evidence' The }ligh Court in its constiiutiorral juriscliction cannot sit as a Court of Appeal' Even otherwise' in the r't'ake of lresh developllents, in terms of facts as well as law laid down in Para 355 of the Muhammadan Law, the f'ather being natural guardian has preferential right of custociy of the rrinors' This being so' the instant petition fails and is dismissed with r-io order as to costs' t, ''''7zl'ml'',4pproved fon RePontft ng /v4f JUDGE (MIR.ZA VIQAS RAUF) J1JDGtr
